content.claim: Poland's delayed-selection reform was politically reversed after ~18 years (PiS government, 2017–2020 phaseout) despite documented achievement gains, demonstrating that structural reforms lacking durable multi-party consensus are vulnerable to reversal and undoing.
content.context: Hungary cannot feasibly implement a structural reform eliminating or significantly delaying early selection before 2030 without (a) substantial prior teacher-workforce stabilization in rural and STEM areas, (b) explicit sequencing away from concurrent National Core Curriculum and AI-integration reforms, and (c) multi-party legislative consensus to protect against political reversal—only the first precondition is currently in progress.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
